===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Gardenia anime.png|thumb|250px|Gardenia in {{aniseries|DP}}]] ====''Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl''==== Gardenia debuted in ''[[DP031|The Grass-Type Is Always Greener]]''. She was first seen unsuccessfully attempting to {{pkmn2|caught|catch}} a {{pkmn2|wild}} {{p|Gloom}} in the [[Eterna Forest]]. She then ran off to see [[Ash's Turtwig]], which she complimented. After that, Gardenia had a short {{pkmn|battle}} with {{Ash}}, revealing to him that the [[Eterna City]] [[Gym Leader]] specialized in {{type|Grass}} {{OBP|Pokémon|species}}, but she refrained from identifying herself as the Gym Leader. During the battle, she sent out {{p|Cherubi}} first, though she soon [[recall]]ed it. She then sent out her own {{p|Turtwig}}, which defeated both Ash's Turtwig and {{AP|Staravia}}. [[File:James and Gardenia.png|thumb|left|220px|James giving Cacnea to Gardenia]] Gardenia informed Ash, {{ashfr}}, and [[Cheryl]] that she knew about a {{p|Combee}} wall near some [[Pomeg Berry]] flowers, and she joined them during their search for the [[Amber Castle]]. The group was confronted by {{TRT}}, but instead of being horrified to learn they were thieves, Gardenia became infatuated with [[James's Carnivine]] and {{TP|James|Cacnea}} instead. She eventually decided to battle the trio after seeing [[Jessie's Seviper]], and also rescued [[Cheryl's Mothim]]. Gardenia later left the group after receiving a message from a {{p|Nuzleaf}} regarding some urgent business. Ash and his friends encountered Gardenia again at the [[Eterna Historical Museum]] in ''[[DP036|A Secret Sphere of Influence!]]''. Gardenia instantly recognized [[Nando]], who had been apprehended by [[Officer Jenny]] on suspicion of theft, from his recent Gym match victory against her. Ash and the others were surprised to learn that Gardenia was in fact the Eterna Gym Leader. Later, Gardenia and her Turtwig helped recover the stolen item in question, the [[Adamant Orb]], which had actually been stolen by Team Rocket. Gardenia had a Gym battle with Ash in ''[[DP037|The Grass Menagerie!]]''. {{AP|Turtwig}} went up against {{p|Cherubi}} first, but it was recalled. Staravia then defeated Cherubi. After Staravia was defeated by Gardenia's Turtwig's {{m|Leech Seed}}, Ash defeated it with his own Turtwig. Next, {{p|Roserade}} defeated Ash's Turtwig with its {{m|Weather Ball}}, but later, it was defeated by {{AP|Aipom}}'s {{m|Swift}} and {{m|Focus Punch}} combination, thus earning Ash the {{Badge|Forest}}. Gardenia visited [[Emeragrove Town]] to promote Pokémon battling in ''[[DP054|Once There Were Greenfields]]''. She took part in a [[Multi Battle|Tag Battle]] alongside [[James]], where they went up against Ash and {{an|Dawn}}. Gardenia's Turtwig and James's Cacnea went up against Ash's Aipom and [[Dawn's Pachirisu]]. Following Gardenia's instructions, James witnessed Cacnea as it used {{m|Drain Punch}} for the first time. Turtwig was taken out first because Gardenia was paying closer attention to Cacnea, and soon, Ash and Dawn emerged victorious. After the battle, she offered to train James's Cacnea and make it stronger, and James reluctantly accepted. She reappeared in a flashback in ''[[DP101|Barry's Busting Out All Over!]]'', where {{an|Barry}} revealed that he had earned the Forest Badge from her. ====[[Pokémon movie]]s==== Gardenia also made a cameo in the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. ====Character==== Gardenia is an optimist and overly cheery. Her battle style is reliant on [[harsh sunlight]], which increases the power of [[move]]s like {{m|Solar Beam}} or {{m|Weather Ball}}, and her Pokémon's incredible speed. Gardenia actively seeks out new challenges, so she often ventures to the [[Eterna Forest]] to {{pkmn2|caught|catch}} new Pokémon that she could train. She is also prepared to [[trade]] for {{type|Grass}}s she feels have potential. Her obsession with Grass Pokémon is overwhelming and causes her to become flustered to such an extent that she did not take {{TRT}} seriously, due to her fascination with [[James's Carnivine]] and {{TP|James|Cacnea}}. ====Pokémon==== {{MissingInfo|2|java|enva|scope=section}} =====On hand===== This listing is of Gardenia's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |main=no |trainer=Gardenia |pkmn=Cherubi |type1=Grass |img=Gardenia Cherubi.png |epnum=DP031 |epname=The Grass-Type Is Always Greener! |vajp=Chinami Nishimura |vaen= |desc=Gardenia's {{p|Cherubi}} was first seen in ''[[DP031|The Grass-Type Is Always Greener!]]'' when Gardenia used it in an unofficial {{pkmn|battle}} against [[Ash's Turtwig]]. It displayed its high speed, but it was revealed that Gardenia was just trying to get a read on Turtwig's abilities before she recalled it. It later appeared during Ash and Gardenia's [[Gym]] battle during ''[[DP037|The Grass Menagerie!]]'', where it was able to outspeed Turtwig's attacks using its {{a|Chlorophyll}} [[Ability]] to enhance its speed and forced Ash to recall him. It then went up against {{AP|Staravia}} where Ash was able to blind it using the sunlight and then Staravia was able to knock it out. Cherubi's known moves are {{m|Magical Leaf}} and {{m|Solar Beam}}, and its Ability is {{a|Chlorophyll}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Gardenia |pkmn=Turtwig |type1=Grass |img=Gardenia Turtwig.png |epnum=DP031 |epname=The Grass-Type Is Always Greener! |vajp=Miyako Itō |vaen=Carter Cathcart{{!}}Billy Beach |desc=Gardenia's {{p|Turtwig}} was first seen when it battled [[Ash's Turtwig]] and {{AP|Staravia}}. It had great speed and power, especially in regards to its Leech Seed attack. It was untouchable by any of Ash's Pokémon and won the match. It helped protect the [[Adamant Orb]] in ''[[DP036|A Secret Sphere of Influence!]]''. During Ash's [[Gym]] battle, Gardenia used its impressive speed to once again defeat Ash's Staravia. However, it lost to Ash's own Turtwig after a fierce round. Turtwig reappeared in ''[[DP054|Once There Were Greenfields!]]'', where it was used in a [[Multi Battle|Tag Battle]] alongside [[James's Cacnea]], but it was defeated by [[Dawn's Pachirisu]]'s {{m|Spark}}. [[Nando's Roselia]] is known to have defeated Turtwig in an earlier battle. Turtwig's known moves are {{m|Leech Seed}}, {{m|Leaf Storm}}, {{m|Bite}}, and {{m|Tackle}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |main=no |trainer=Gardenia |pkmn=Roserade |type1=Grass |type2=Poison |img=Gardenia Roserade.png |epnum=DP037 |epname=The Grass Menagerie! |vajp= |vaen=Sarah Natochenny |desc={{p|Roserade}} was the final Pokémon used by Gardenia against {{Ash}}. It was able to easily knock out Turtwig using a {{m|Weather Ball}} powered by the bright sun. When it went up against Ash's {{TP|Dawn|Aipom}}, it was unable to keep up with the active Pokémon. It was eventually knocked out with a combination of {{m|Focus Punch}} and {{m|Swift}}. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[DP183|League Unleashed!]]''. Roserade briefly appeared alongside Gardenia during the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. Roserade's known moves are {{m|Grass Knot}}, {{m|Weather Ball}}, {{m|Magical Leaf}}, and {{m|Flash}}.}} =====Mentioned===== In ''[[DP031|The Grass-Type is Always Greener!]]'', Gardenia mentioned that she has a {{p|Bellossom}}, a {{p|Vileplume}}, and a {{p|Victreebel}} when she wanted to [[trade]] one of her Pokémon for [[James's Carnivine]]. {| style="float:left; width: 96px" |- | {{InactivePoké|Gardenia|Bellossom|182Bellossom.png|grass|size=96px|link=no}} | {{InactivePoké|Gardenia|Vileplume|045Vileplume.png|grass|poison|size=96px|link=no}} | {{InactivePoké|Gardenia|Victreebel|071Victreebel.png|grass|poison|size=96px|link=no}} |}{{-}} =====Training===== {{TrainerPoké |main=yes |trainer=James |pkmn=Cacnea |type1=Grass |img=Gardenia Cacnea.png |epnum=AG006 |epname=A Poached Ego! |vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi |vaen=Michele Knotz |desc=Gardenia first met {{p|Cacnea}} in ''[[DP031|The Grass-Type Is Always Greener!]]'', when it was sent out by [[James]]. Gardenia was automatically attracted to it, and she asked it to scratch her back by using {{m|Pin Missile}}. Later, in ''[[DP054|Once There Were Greenfields]]'', after battling alongside James and Cacnea in a Tag Battle, Gardenia knew that Cacnea had the ability to learn {{m|Drain Punch}}, so she offered to take on Cacnea to help it reach its full potential. After much soul searching James agreed and handed Cacnea over. Gardenia soon helped Cacnea master Drain Punch and she used it in a Gym battle against {{an|Barry}}, where it proved to be a strong opponent, unlike its many battles under James's command.}} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{grass color}}|bordercolor={{grass color light}} |ja=本多知恵子 ''[[Chieko Honda]]'' |en=Lara Starr Rigores |fi=Henna Haverinen ([[DP031]]-[[DP037]])
